subjects: [clarify][hermes-tailchat-example] Need decision on sidecar review trigger
[escalation][hermes-tailchat-example] Human decision needed on merge gate semantics
[memory-request][hermes-tailchat-example] Need historical rationale for review policy
[decision][hermes-tailchat-example] Use implementation bead as thread anchor
[policy][hermes-tailchat-example] Sidecar review required for non-trivial changes
[escalate-human][hermes-tailchat-example] Need maintainer choice on branch protection

body.state: Worker claimed the bead and is starting with the spec and fixture slice.
body.recommended_next_step: Proceed with schema-first changes and request review once tests pass

body.state: The contract docs are drafted and the remaining work is fixture verification.
findings: The implementation bead should remain the only routine thread anchor
Subject prefixes split cleanly into three routing classes
risks: Future helpers may drift if they embed their own prefix lists
body.recommended_next_step: Reviewer should validate fixture coverage against the contract before approving

body.goal: Review AGENTS guidance and message-fixture docs
body.state: Implementation committed locally with bead refs
body.ask: Confirm guidance completeness and identify any missing review/test traceability rules
body.recommended_next_step: Reviewer should approve or request changes and reference the review bead in the reply

body.goal: Return review findings before the slice closes
body.state: The contract and fixture coverage are good, but the review bead linkage still needs to be mentioned in the done example.
body.recommended_next_step: Amend the done fixture to mention review disposition linkage

body.goal: Resolve a collision before review continues
body.state: Reviewer found that the worker and another agent are editing the same traceability helper without explicit coordination.
body.ask: Pause new edits and either reserve the shared files or move to an isolated worktree
body.recommended_next_step: Coordinate in-thread, then resume review after the collision is resolved

body.goal: Close the sidecar review bead after a non-blocking pass
body.state: Reviewer checked the implementation bead, review bead, commit refs, and tests.
body.recommended_next_step: Implementation bead may proceed to final close once .beads is synced and PR notes reference this review disposition

clarify.subject: [clarify][hermes-tailchat-example] Need decision on sidecar review trigger

body.goal: Implement AGENTS guidance for sidecar review beads
body.state: Worker has updated role guidance and design docs but needs a policy answer for trivial slices.
body.ambiguity: Should trivial docs-only bead updates require a sidecar review bead?
options: require sidecar review only for non-trivial changes
skip sidecar review when a change is docs-only and low risk
body.recommended_option: require sidecar review only for non-trivial changes
body.impact_if_unanswered: Workers may either over-create review beads or silently skip needed reviews
body.recommended_next_step: Hermes should answer with the repo-wide threshold so tests and helpers can enforce it consistently

escalation.subject: [escalation][hermes-tailchat-example] Human decision needed on merge gate semantics

body.goal: Resolve a product-risk decision that workers cannot settle locally
body.state: The worker found two viable branch-protection interpretations with different shipping friction.
body.risk: Choosing the wrong merge gate semantics could weaken review guarantees or slow solo-maintainer velocity unnecessarily.
options: require human approval for every non-trivial change
permit auto-merge after sidecar review and required checks
body.recommended_option: permit auto-merge after sidecar review and required checks
body.recommended_next_step: Hermes should either answer from existing policy or escalate a concise decision request to the maintainer

body.goal: Capture a reusable process lesson for multi-agent completion evidence
body.state: A worker tried to mark an implementation bead done before the required PR artifact existed.
body.problem: Agents marked beads done before opening a PR/MR required by repo policy
body.resolution: Codex worker guidance now states a bead is not done without required git artifact evidence
body.reusable_rule: Definition of done must include the required git artifact, not just code changes
body.recommended_next_step: Hermes should update durable guidance and keep the rule discoverable for future workers

memory_request.subject: [memory-request][hermes-tailchat-example] Need historical rationale for review policy

body.goal: Recover historical context before changing the workflow helper
body.state: Current docs require review, but the original tradeoff between shipping speed and branch protection is not obvious from the repo alone.
body.question: Why was sidecar review chosen as an additional readiness gate instead of replacing protected-branch review?
body.why_now: The helper implementation needs to decide whether to model sidecar review as sufficient or supplemental evidence.
body.recommended_next_step: Hermes should search memory/docs and answer with the rationale or escalate if the rationale is unavailable

decision.subject: [decision][hermes-tailchat-example] Use implementation bead as thread anchor

body.goal: Answer a workflow clarification without escalating to the human
body.state: Hermes reviewed the design and repo policy docs.
body.decision: Keep the implementation bead as the thread anchor and treat review bead IDs as related metadata.
body.recommended_next_step: Update fixtures and helper tests to enforce the single-thread rule

policy.subject: [policy][hermes-tailchat-example] Sidecar review required for non-trivial changes

body.goal: State the governing rule for closeability checks
body.state: Hermes confirmed the repo policy and AGENTS guidance align on this threshold.
body.policy_rule: Non-trivial slices require sidecar review evidence before final close.
body.recommended_next_step: Bake the rule into helpers and fixture-based tests

body.goal: Keep the implementation slice small and parallelizable
body.state: The current bead mixes schema formalization, policy enforcement, and integration harness work.
body.reason_for_split: The contract can land first and unblock helpers and harnesses without bundling later enforcement logic.
body.recommended_next_step: Create or continue the child beads and keep this thread anchored to the implementation bead

escalate_human.subject: [escalate-human][hermes-tailchat-example] Need maintainer choice on branch protection

body.goal: Request a maintainer decision on a policy-sensitive tradeoff
body.state: Repo docs allow required review and checks, but the exact auto-merge tolerance still needs maintainer confirmation.
body.decision_request: Should solo-maintainer auto-merge remain allowed after sidecar review and required checks, or should human confirmation remain mandatory for every non-trivial slice?
options: keep auto-merge allowed after real review + required checks
require manual maintainer confirmation for all non-trivial merges
body.recommended_option: keep auto-merge allowed after real review + required checks
body.recommended_next_step: Human should choose the preferred merge gate so Hermes can answer future clarifications consistently
